title: CHIPS G16 Python die — coding and debugging for Hostess 7

chosen_die.label: G16 Python / gpy-16 die
chosen_die.reason: Hostess 7 stack is Python-first on pathlib panels; G16 discern routes Python through the CHIPS compiler lane with field_opt safety mandate.

introduction: I code on the CHIPS G16 Python die. Every change starts in the virtual workspace — never live INSTALL first. I py_compile, smoke-run, debug, then promote.
workflow: mirror — copy target module into hostess7-virtual/install/
edit — write only in virtual mirror
smoke — run module json/status via CHIPS gpy die or pythong with HOSTESS7_VIRTUAL_ROOT
debug — read stderr, fix in virtual, re-test
promote — copy virtual → live only with confirm PROMOTE

topics.what: The virtual workspace is a copy of editable modules under NEXUS_STATE_DIR/hostess7-virtual/install/. Live INSTALL is read-only for Hostess 7 self-edits until promote passes all gates.
topics.why: A bad edit to hostess7-brain-guard.py or plate meld consumers can corrupt the whole stack mid-tick. Virtual die isolates failure.
topics.how: hostess7-virtual-workspace.py mirror lib/hostess7-tasklist.py → edit virtual copy → test → promote with confirm PROMOTE.
topics.pitfalls: Editing live lib/*.py directly; promoting without testing-center; skipping brain-guard after promote.

topics.what: py_compile.compile() validates syntax without executing module top-level side effects beyond compile.
topics.why: First gate before any smoke run — catches typos before CHIPS die executes.
topics.how: python3 -m py_compile virtual/install/lib/hostess7-*.py or virtual_workspace test action.
topics.pitfalls: Assuming import-time side effects are safe; only syntax is checked.

topics.what: CHIPS G16 Python die runs the virtual module with json/status argv — same contract as every lib/*.py panel module.
topics.why: Proves the module boots under field_opt env without touching live STATE panels.
topics.pitfalls: Using live STATE while testing virtual INSTALL; hardcoded paths ignoring env.

topics.what: Debug means: run virtual module, capture stderr/stdout, fix in virtual mirror only, re-run compile + smoke until ok.
topics.why: CHIPS die exposes real stack traces — I read them against programming-explain topics (atomic write, safe_json_load, INSTALL/STATE).
topics.how: 1) test fails → read error 2) edit virtual file 3) py_compile 4) smoke json 5) ledger row in virtual-workspace-ledger.jsonl
topics.pitfalls: Patching live tree to 'fix fast'; skipping ledger witness; debugging without virtual mirror.
topics.example: Traceback in _load → check path is STATE not INSTALL doctrine

topics.what: After Python self-edit promotes, optional G16 native recompile refreshes CHIPS C/C++ dies — via AmmoLang g16_recompile task only.
topics.why: Python panel modules do not need full G16 rebuild; native Queen/CHIPS changes do.
topics.how: lib/ammolang-run.sh g16_recompile — only after virtual Python gates pass.
topics.pitfalls: Running g16_recompile before virtual Python tests; bypassing AmmoLang router.

topics.what: Promote copies one vetted virtual file to live INSTALL after py_compile, smoke, codecraft testing-center, and confirm phrase PROMOTE.
topics.why: Operator authority + automated gates — she does not silently rewrite production.
topics.how: dispatch {action: promote, path: lib/hostess7-operator.py, confirm: PROMOTE}
topics.pitfalls: Promote without confirm; promoting forbidden paths (MANIFEST, plate-meld).
